name: asi1-code
description: Sidebar AI chat for VS Code powered by ASI1 — streaming replies, markdown, and workspace file hints.
author: Gautam Manak

asiAssistant.apiKey.description: ASI1 API key (optional if ASI_ONE_API_KEY is set in the environment)

asiAssistant.imageBaseUrl.description: Optional override for image API base (e.g. https://api.asi1.ai/v1). Empty derives from asiAssistant.baseUrl.

asiAssistant.imageModel.description: Model id for image/generate. Empty falls back to asiAssistant.model.

asiAssistant.systemPrompt.default:
You are a coding assistant in VS Code/Cursor. Use ONLY standard GitHub-flavored markdown: real triple-backtick fences with a language tag (e.g. ```markdown, ```sql, ```bash). Never output the words MARKDOWN or CODE as fake fences. For a full .md document, put it inside a ```markdown fence, then on a new line after the closing fence write: Save as `filename.md`. For code files use ```ts / ```py etc. and Save as `path/file.ext`. Tables, lists, and headings are welcome.

Fetch.ai / ASI:One / Agentverse: When the user asks to create Agentverse agents, uAgents, or ASI1 chat-profile compatible agents, tell them to run the command **ASI: Create Agentverse uAgent Project** from the Command Palette — it scaffolds the same folder layout as the `create-agentverse-agent` CLI (`main.py` with uAgents + Agent Chat Protocol from `uagents_core.contrib.protocols.chat`, `agent.py` with the `chat` hook, Makefile, Docker, `.env`). Alternatively they can use `pip install create-agentverse-agent` and run `create-agentverse-agent` locally. Explain that they should set `AGENTVERSE_API_KEY` in `.env` for Agentverse registration, keep `AGENT_SEED_PHRASE` secret, and use `make dev` / `make run` per the generated README. Official Fetch uAgents are Python — do not scaffold or recommend JavaScript/TypeScript uAgent servers; use the Python command and templates only.
asiAssistant.systemPrompt.description: System message prepended to every conversation

asiAssistant.streamResponse.description: Stream the model reply (SSE). Ignored while built-in tools are enabled (tools use non-streaming rounds).

asiAssistant.webSearch.description: Send ASI:One web_search so the model can use live web results (see OpenAI compatibility docs).

asiAssistant.enableTools.description: Enable client-side tools (read/search workspace files) with ASI:One tool calling. When off, only web_search + chat apply.

asiAssistant.agenticSession.description: Send x-session-id for Agentverse / agentic flows (ASI:One). Uses sessionId if set, otherwise a per-chat random id until you Clear.

asiAssistant.sessionId.description: Optional fixed x-session-id (overrides auto). Empty uses a generated id when agentic session is on.

asiAssistant.maxToolRounds.description: Max API rounds when resolving tool calls (each round may invoke multiple tools).

asiAssistant.autoApplyFiles.description: Automatically write detected files to the open workspace after each reply (requires a folder open). Set false to only use “Create files”.

asiAssistant.bannerTitle.description: Optional title next to the banner logo (leave empty to show logo only)

asiAssistant.bannerSubtitle.description: Short line under the banner title (leave empty to hide)

asiAssistant.bannerLogoUrl.description: Optional https URL for the banner logo. Empty uses the bundled ASI1 Code logo.
